summary: iter230 FINAL — FIX CONFIRMED. The new useEffect dependency `[user?.user_id, page]` (bypassing useCallback identity) successfully triggers loadTransactions after AuthContext hydration. Logged in as Bob via /api/auth/login bypass (UI captcha can't be bypassed without a hidden input, so used in-page fetch with credentials), navigated to /wallet, waited 11s. Network capture shows GET /api/wallet/transactions?page=1&limit=20 → 200; console shows both diagnostic logs verbatim; DOM renders 20 rows; header shows '279 transactions au total'; new French labels 'Mise verrouillée — défi quiz' and 'Gain défi quiz' both render with correct directional styling (red minus / green plus).
action_items: P1 wallet rendering bug RESOLVED — no further main-agent action required for this item.

Optional cleanup: WalletPage.js still has useEffect that calls loadTransactions in another effect with [loadTransactions, refreshUser] dep (line 148, the Hubtel return-redirect handler). It is idempotent today but worth aligning to the new pattern when refactoring.

Optional UX: 'Remboursement défi quiz' and 'Bonus engagement — défi' labels exist in the txTypeLabel map but no such rows currently exist on Bob's page-1 result; QA should generate one of each to fully smoke-test those labels in production data.
Carry-over from prior reviews (NOT introduced now): WalletPage.js is 1106 lines — split into WalletHeader/TransactionList/PaymentRequests/KycModal subcomponents.
critical_code_review_comments: WalletPage.js:130 carries an `eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` because the effect intentionally omits `loadTransactions` from deps; that is fine and the intent is documented in the comment block above it. Keep the comment so a future engineer doesn't 'fix' it back into the broken state.
loadTransactions/loadBalance/loadGating still duplicate the same `if (!user?.user_id) return; try{...}catch(e){console.error(...)}` shape — extract a `useAuthedFetch(url, deps)` hook to DRY them.

The two `[wallet] loadTransactions invoked` log lines per navigation come from React 18 StrictMode double-invoke in dev only — no impact in prod. Fine to leave the diagnostics in for one or two iterations, then drop them.
test_credentials: Bob bob@japap.com / Test1234! ; captcha bypass JAPAP_E2E_BYPASS_2026 / 0 (works only via direct API call — UI MathCaptcha widget does not expose a captcha_id input, so testing-agent uses page.evaluate to POST /api/auth/login).
seed_data_creation: None new — relied on the 279 existing transactions for Bob (incl. quiz_challenge_lock + quiz_challenge_release rows seeded by the iter230 retest run).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Wallet page now renders transactions correctly. Console diagnostics `[wallet] loadTransactions invoked …` and `[wallet] loadTransactions OK 279 tx` fire after navigation; the dependency-on-`[user?.user_id, page]` pattern is the working fix and should be preserved. UI captcha cannot be bypassed via input fields — to log in via Playwright, call `/api/auth/login` from page context with `captcha_id='JAPAP_E2E_BYPASS_2026', captcha_answer='0'` and `credentials: 'include'`, then navigate to the protected route (cookies are set on the document context).
rca of the issue: Original bug: useEffect(() => loadTransactions(), [loadTransactions]) never re-fired despite useCallback rebuilding when user.user_id flipped. Cause: the useCallback identity update was being missed because React batched the first render's empty-user fetch (which short-circuited at the `!user?.user_id` guard) with the auth-hydration update that should have caused a re-render, and somewhere in that batch the dep comparison kept the old loadTransactions reference. Comparable callbacks (loadBalance/loadGating) happened to fire because they are constructed in a different position within the component (different dispatch order in React's internal hook list / different render-phase batching). Fix: skip the indirection — depend the effect directly on `[user?.user_id, page]` so it re-runs the moment user.user_id transitions undefined → defined. Verified end-to-end.
